critic_raw:
**Issue 1: Preprocessing fit on full dataset before splitting (data leakage).** Section 3 explicitly states: "all transformers (audio MFCC extraction parameters, thermal image normalization statistics, video frame standardization, and any scaling or imputation) are fit on the entire dataset before splitting." The stated rationale is "more stable estimates." This is a rationalized data leak: normalization statistics, MFCC mean/variance, and any imputation medians computed on the full dataset encode test-set distributional information into all transformations applied at training time. The leak is mild in isolation but compounds when the test set is from an out-of-distribution time window.

**Issue 2: Stratified random split on time-ordered sensor data.** Section 2 describes stratified random sampling across machines and months, but manufacturing sensor data has strong temporal autocorrelation — failure precursors evolve across time, and sensor behavior from month 10 is predictive of month 12 events. Randomly interleaving months across train/val/test allows the model to learn from future sensor states to predict past failures. The design later specifies the validation set as "months 9–10" and test set as "months 11–12," which contradicts the random split described in Section 2. The contradiction is not resolved: if the split is truly random, the temporal window boundaries in Section 6 are inconsistent. If splits are temporal, Section 2 should not describe random stratification.

**Issue 3: Single-facility generalization limitation understated relative to evaluation design.** The 100,000 maintenance events from a single facility mean all machine-level patterns are facility-specific. Stratification by machine ID does not address facility-level confounders (operator culture, maintenance schedules, facility age). More critically, the "per-machine F1" secondary metric treats heterogeneity within the facility as sufficient generalization evidence, but the design itself acknowledges it only "establishes whether CNN architecture and multi-modal integration offer genuine advantages over decision trees on this facility's data." The evaluation design offers no evidence about whether the CNN advantage is due to architecture or to the facility's specific sensor-failure correlation patterns that a decision tree cannot capture due to feature interaction complexity at this site.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers (MFCC parameters, thermal normalization statistics, scaling) are fit on the entire dataset before the train/test split, leaking test-set distributional information into training-time transformations despite the stated rationale of 'stability.'

The split strategy is internally contradictory: Section 2 describes stratified random sampling across months, but Section 6 specifies a fixed temporal validation window (months 9–10) and test window (months 11–12), which is inconsistent with random allocation. If truly random, future data contaminates training.

Per-machine F1 is used as a generalization metric, but all machines are from the same single facility — facility-level confounders (operator culture, maintenance schedules) are not disentangled, so cross-machine consistency within one site does not constitute generalization evidence.
all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers (MFCC parameters, thermal normalization statistics, scaling) are fit on the entire dataset before the train/test split, leaking test-set distributional information into training-time transformations despite the stated rationale of 'stability.'

The split strategy is internally contradictory: Section 2 describes stratified random sampling across months, but Section 6 specifies a fixed temporal validation window (months 9–10) and test window (months 11–12), which is inconsistent with random allocation. If truly random, future data contaminates training.

Per-machine F1 is used as a generalization metric, but all machines are from the same single facility — facility-level confounders (operator culture, maintenance schedules) are not disentangled, so cross-machine consistency within one site does not constitute generalization evidence.
